title: carex pilulifera var. azorica
intro: Carex pilulifera var. azorica, also known as Azorean Pill Sedge, is a rare and endemic species of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is native to the island of Flores in the Azores, Portugal. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, and has been shown to have potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving immune function.
